name: Issue Triage Automation
description: Triage, label, and manage GitHub issues efficiently using CLI commands for faster project management.

steps.title: List issues by label
steps.description: View open issues filtered by a specific label.

steps.title: Create an issue with labels and assignee
steps.description: File a new issue with metadata pre-filled.
steps.command:
gh issue create --title 'Bug: login fails on Safari' --label bug,high-priority --assignee @me --body 'Steps to reproduce:
1. Open Safari
2. Navigate to /login
3. Submit form'

steps.title: Batch-label untriaged issues
steps.description: Add a label to multiple issues at once using a loop.
steps.command: gh issue list --label '' --state open --json number --jq '.[].number' | head -10 | xargs -I{} gh issue edit {} --add-label needs-triage
steps.tip: Filter carefully before batch-labeling to avoid mislabeling issues.

steps.title: Close stale issues with a comment
steps.description: Close old issues that have not had activity, with an explanatory comment.
steps.command: gh issue close 123 --comment 'Closing due to inactivity. Please reopen if this is still relevant.'

steps.title: Transfer an issue to another repository
steps.description: Move an issue to a more appropriate repository.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Issue Triage Automation
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Issue Summary ==="
echo "Open bugs: $(gh issue list --label bug --state open --json number --jq 'length')"
echo "Open features: $(gh issue list --label enhancement --state open --json number --jq 'length')"
echo "Untriaged: $(gh issue list --state open --json labels --jq '[.[] | select(.labels | length == 0)] | length')"

echo ""
echo "=== Recent Issues ==="
gh issue list --state open --limit 10

echo ""
echo "=== Triage Commands ==="
echo "Label:    gh issue edit <number> --add-label bug"
echo "Assign:   gh issue edit <number> --add-assignee @me"
echo "Close:    gh issue close <number> --comment 'reason'"
echo "Transfer: gh issue transfer <number> owner/repo"
echo "Pin:      gh issue pin <number>"

faq.question: How do I create issue templates?
faq.answer: Create files in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ directory. Use YAML frontmatter for structured templates with labels, assignees, and form fields.

faq.question: Can I automate issue triage with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. Use actions like actions/labeler for auto-labeling based on file paths, or create custom workflows triggered by issue events.
